ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT code identified for low fat flavoured milk as a food product; SNOMED CT coverage for processed dairy beverages is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of low fat flavoured milk is approximately 200 ml (about 206 g), equivalent to one standard drinking glass or a small carton.

nutrient_source.title: Low Fat Flavoured Milk — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for low fat flavoured milk (cr_id F01030009). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for flavoured low fat milk
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 34 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for low fat flavoured milk (chocolate milk, low fat, analogous entry). Flavoured low fat milks consistently show GI in the range 24–40 due to the lactose and protein matrix slowing glucose absorption. GL per serving calculated as: GI (34) × carbohydrate per serving (11.6 g × 2.06 serving factor = 23.9 g) / 100 = 8.1. This is an analogous estimate; the exact Singapore HPB product formulation was not independently tested in the University of Sydney database.

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar (10.5 g/100 g) with minimal fibre (0.3 g/100 g); sodium is low at 25 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is low (25 mg/100 g), the high added sugar content (10.5 g/100 g) independently elevates blood pressure risk via insulin-driven sodium retention and sympathetic nervous system activation (AHA 2018); individuals with hypertension should limit flavoured milk and prefer plain unsweetened low fat milk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (10.5 g/100 g) with negligible fibre (0.3 g/100 g) and liquid form accelerating glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: The 10.5 g/100 g sugar load with only 0.3 g/100 g dietary fibre provides no meaningful glycaemic buffering; despite a low GI of approximately 34, the liquid delivery and high sugar density make this unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes — plain unsweetened low fat milk or variants with less than 5 g sugar/100 g are strongly preferred.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.9 g/100 g) is favourable, but high added sugar (10.5 g/100 g) raises triglyceride and LDL risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Low fat flavoured milk has a favourable saturated fat profile (~0.9 g/100 g) and no trans fat, which supports cardiovascular health; however, the high added sugar content (10.5 g/100 g) can raise triglycerides and contribute to atherogenic dyslipidaemia — individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose plain low fat milk to retain the calcium and protein benefits without the sugar burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Calcium (78 mg/100 g) and protein (4.4 g/100 g) support bone protection; low saturated fat and no trans fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Low fat flavoured milk provides calcium and dairy protein that support bone protection in rheumatoid arthritis; saturated fat is low and trans fat is absent, but the added sugar (10.5 g/100 g) may contribute to low-grade inflammation — limiting portion size and preferring plain low fat milk is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Low fat flavoured milk is a processed dairy beverage made from pasteurised and homogenised skimmed or reduced-fat cow's milk with added sugar and natural or artificial flavourings such as chocolate, strawberry, or vanilla. It is typically consumed chilled and has a smooth, creamy texture with a sweet taste characteristic of its added flavour. It contains approximately 1.4 g fat per 100 g, making it significantly lower in fat than full-cream milk, while retaining a good protein content of 4.4 g/100 g and calcium at 78 mg/100 g. The energy density is moderate at 77 kcal/100 g. The primary nutritional concern is the high added sugar content of 10.5 g/100 g with minimal dietary fibre (0.3 g/100 g), which provides little glycaemic buffering despite the food's low glycemic index of approximately 34. Clinically, this product is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension due to the sugar load, and those with cardiovascular disease should prefer plain low fat milk to avoid triglyceride elevation. For individuals with rheumatoid arthritis, the calcium and protein content are beneficial for bone health, though portion control is advised. In Singapore, low fat flavoured milk is widely available in supermarkets and convenience stores as a packaged beverage, commonly consumed as a snack or post-exercise drink. It is suitable for vegetarians and is generally halal-certified. The product is classified as a processed dairy food at moderate concern level for metabolic conditions.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F01030009) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. The source is a primary approved regional database for Singapore foods. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for low fat flavoured milk, which is the closest approved GI source entry.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for low fat flavoured milk is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this product (F01030009) as of 2026. potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this product. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: confirmed negligible/absent in low fat dairy milk per HPB and USDA FoodData Central data for equivalent products. saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.9 g/100 g based on typical fat composition of low fat dairy milk (approximately 65% of total fat as saturated fat), consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for low fat flavoured milk. GI of 34 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for low fat flavoured milk (chocolate milk, low fat); the exact Singapore HPB product was not independently GI-tested. GL per serving calculated using 200 ml serving (206 g): carbohydrate = 11.6 g/100 g × 2.06 = 23.9 g; GL = 34 × 23.9 / 100 = 8.1.
